entry.title: Published API for simulating supplier invoice posting in S4/HANA private edition
entry.content:
Hi All,

Are there any published APIs or ways where we can simulate the posting of a supplier Invoice in MIRO or FB60 or ? Our client has an external system through which the supplier invoice data would be posted and prior to that would want to simulate the posting. Any suggestions would be really appreciated.

Regards,
Shravan Chintapatla

Regards,

Bharanidharan S P M

entry.title: Re: SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model: Modeling Update (Join)
entry.content:
@Paulo_Moncada

As previously mentioned, the field sequence remains a concern in our current SAP PaPM development. We are unable to adjust the field order as required, which is affecting the usability and flexibility of our modeling efforts.

I kindly request that you review this issue along with the other points raised earlier. The way the recent changes are being reflected from the standard configuration is proving to be quite inconvenient for our development process.

Your support in resolving these matters would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: • SAP Note 2348017 e • SAP Note 2220005
entry.content:
Porque não perguntam aos vossos administradores SAP? Podem até pedir-lhes um S-ID para poderem procurá-las e consultá-las vocês mesmos.

  • 2348017 - MESSAGE_TYPE_NOT_VALID é criado durante o processamento de uma transação
  • 2220005 - S/4 HANA: modificações do modelo de dados na determinação de preços e na técnica de condições

entry.title: GenericTile aggregation actionButtons doesn't work
entry.content:
Hi,

I've been trying to add the aggregation actionButtons to an exixting sap.m.GenericTile but I can not see any button added. 

Here's my code :

<GenericTile header="Header" mode="ActionMode">

                    <tileContent>
                        <TileContent>
                            <ImageContent src="sap-icon://product"/>
                        </TileContent>
                    </tileContent>

                    <actionButtons>
                            <Button type="Transparent" icon="sap-icon://delete" press="onDeletePress" />
                    </actionButtons>

                </GenericTile>
 
the version of mine UI is 1.108. ActionMode is supposed to be valid since 1.96
 
any help would be apprecieted 🙂

entry.title: オンライン帳票出力時の印刷設定画面について
entry.content:
こんにちは。

Crystal Reportsにてオンライン帳票出力時、印刷設定画面が2つ起動する事象が発生しました。
当事象について、心当たりのある方がいましたら解決策を教えていただきたいです。

entry.title: Re: Getting error in SAP Integration Suite IFLOW - Unreso...
entry.content:
Hi Ramesh

Thanks

Gopal

entry.title: Re: Mandatory document attachment tab in Manage Certifica...
entry.content:
Hello @RishikeshMishra 

There’s no standard control in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to enforce mandatory attachments before status change to Manage Certificate Receipts.

Possible workaround: You can implement a side-by-side extension via SAP BTP to introduce validation logic before allowing status updates.

System-side enforcement via core config isn’t available; only an SAP Influence Request can address this natively.

Thanks and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: SAP Information Steward Task Failing - "Invalid column name 'IS_GEN_SOURCE_ROWID' " Error
entry.content:
Hi,

We are facing an issue with a task in SAP Information Steward.

Error : 

error message for operation <SQLExecute>: <[Microsoft][ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server][SQL
Server]Invalid column name 'IS_GEN_SOURCE_ROWID'.
[Microsoft][ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server][SQL Server]Could not use view or function 'CMD007C_D_P4821T5004_FD' because of
binding errors.

Observation:

  1. The column 'IS_GEN_SOURCE_ROWID' is missing in prod which matches the error
  2. Interestingly, the same column is also missing in INTEG, but the task runs fine there without errors.

entry.title: RAP Transactional app: performance considerations for singleton pattern in draft enabled RAP BO
entry.content:
Introduction

When developing a transactional app, you can use the singleton pattern for a draft enabled RAP BO to enable editing / inline create on the actual root entity.

In this blog, you will learn how the singleton pattern impacts performance and other advantages and disadvantages of this pattern in the context of Fiori elements.

This blog is relevant for

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition  
  • SAP BTP ABAP environment  

Further reading:

  • Related blog posts
  • Learn how you can use ABAP technology to develop innovative applications and business solutions across SAP’s portfolio on SAP Learning Site.

Example BO

Our data model consists of a table T1 and a corresponding text table T1_TXT.

@EndUserText.label : 'Tab1'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #C
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #ALLOWED
define table T1 {
  key client            : abap.clnt not null;
  key k1                : zpw_d1 not null;
  f1                    : abap.char(20);
  f2                    : abap.char(20);
  f3                    : abap.char(20);
  f4                    : abap.char(20);
  f5                    : abap.char(20);
  f6                    : abap.char(20);
  f7                    : abap.char(20);
  f8                    : abap.char(20);
  last_changed_at       : abp_lastchange_tstmpl;
  local_last_changed_at : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl;
}
@EndUserText.label : 'Tab1 Text'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #C
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #ALLOWED
define table T1_TXT {
  key client            : abap.clnt not null;
  key langu             : abap.lang not null;
  @AbapCatalog.foreignKey.keyType : #TEXT_KEY
  @AbapCatalog.foreignKey.screenCheck : false
  key k1                : zpw_d1 not null
    with foreign key T1
      where client = T1_TXT.client
        and k1 = T1_TXT.k1;
  description           : abap.char(30);
  local_last_changed_at : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl;
}

Therefore, the draft-enabled RAP BO with singleton pattern consists of the singleton root entity type, T1 entity type, and T1_TXT entity type, each with a draft table.

Singleton draft

When the Edit action is performed on a root entity, a corresponding draft entity including all subentities is created. In the singleton pattern, this basically means that a draft entity is created for all entities of the RAP BO.
Typically, the Edit action is performed when the user clicks the Edit button in the Fiori elements app. You can also use the following ABAP EML statement:

MODIFY ENTITY zi_t1_s
  EXECUTE edit
  FROM VALUE #( ( %cid = |CID1|  singletonid = 1 ) ).
COMMIT ENTITIES.

For our example BO, the Edit action therefore consists of three SQL statements in the form of INSERT INTO <draft table> SELECT FROM <entity type cds view>. No data is passed to the ABAP application server, only one statement per entity type, regardless of the number of entities.

The following measurement was performed in an ABAP Platform performance test system with respect to the above SQL statements as total:

#Rows T1#Rows T1_TXTHANA CPU time [ms]HANA max memory consumption [mb]
104071
100400753
100040001356
100004000081045
1000004000007600437

This table shows the performance impact of the singleton pattern compared to a RAP BO without a singleton pattern. It is linear, so you can extrapolate for a higher number of lines.

If you use ABAP EML to modify entities, you do not need to create a draft entity, so this penalty does not necessarily apply to ABAP EML statements.

The runtime behavior when changing a draft entity or in the action prepare is not affected by the singleton pattern.

During the activation action, the draft entities are discarded using a DELETE statement:

#Rows T1#Rows T1_TXTHANA CPU time [ms]HANA max memory consumption [mb]
104071
10040072
10004000274
100004000015510
10000040000043043

So you can add this to the performance impact.

Logically, these numbers depend on the table and CDS view design as well as the system. But the basic statement remains.

If the user changes more than about 10% of the entities in the interaction phase, the singleton pattern is more performant because draft entities do not always have to be created.

Singleton in Fiori Elements

Using a singleton RAP BO pattern in Fiori elements has the following characteristics:

  • Multi-line edit/inline creation for the actual root entity
  • The app can temporarily store global information in the singleton draft entity
  • All entities of the RAP BO are locked for a user
  • You cannot use selection fields as the filter bar is only available in the list report. Alternatively, you can add search capabilities to the entity. A search field is then available 

See link on how to create a Fiori Elements app for a RAP BO with singleton pattern.

Custom Business Configurations

When you create business configurations app using the ABAP RAP programming model, you use the Custom Business Configurations app and the business configuration maintenance object ADT wizard.

The RAP BO that this wizard generates always uses the singleton pattern for the following reasons:

  • For configuration tables, you want to have a spreadsheet like user experience
  • The customizing transport request can be selected once for all entities and stored in the singleton draft entity
  • Locking the entire configuration for only one user is beneficial. You do not want multiple users to work on the same configuration table in parallel
  • The performance penalty for the singleton pattern is acceptable because
    • Configuration tables rarely have more than 1000 rows, let alone more than 10000 rows
    • Most of the configuration takes place in the intended system/client, not in the production client
    • The frequency of configuration changes is low
    • There is no performance impact when using ABAP EML or the Upload Business Configurations app

The Custom Business Configurations app also supports RAP BO without singleton pattern:

  1. Create the RAP BO / Service binding without using the business configuration maintenance object ADT wizard
  2. Add the relevant transport related functionality to the RAP BO
  3. Create the business configuration maintenance object (SMBC) manually. Leave the option "Skip root entity list report" unchecked

Conclusion

Do not use the singleton pattern if the RAP BO handles more than 50000 entities and the app is used frequently.

entry.title: Re: SAP Query - Issue with missing fields on Selection Sc...
entry.content: Did you follow steps of 3153170 - ME23N tcode Search is not translated ?

entry.title: Re: Loading Master and Transactional Data from Datasphere...
entry.content:
Hi, yes it is also for transactional data indeed. Explained in more detail here https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/43509d67b8b84e66a30851e832f66911/f589cdea41674badaecfa1bf02571b6f.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=cloud When it comes to best practices, SAP doesn’t officially declare ( at least I couldn’t see it ) as a black and white recommendation yet. Acquiring actuals from source system is for sure an option available even in SAP learnings as well along with DSP.

However, If your client already has DSP, having actual data in DSP even just for planning purpose has many advantages ( would be even more powerful along with seamless planning ) and it also aligns with SAP’s strategic direction to position Datasphere as the central harmonization layer within SAP BDC.

- Future proof architecture (Potential live data connection of SAC Planning model with SAP DSP to consume actuals for plan use- no timeline )

- Advanced transformation capabilities for actual data ( SAC is not a DW tool so has limited transformation capabilities )

- Advanced ML capabilities for potential future use cases via databricks ( even if it is not your requirement for today, it may be in the future )

Still there are limitations & prerequisites explained here https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/seamless-planning-between-sap-analytics-cloud-and-sap-datasphere-is-coming/ba-p/13877664

entry.title: Can not find app - Edit Actual Costing Runs (CKMLCP)
entry.content:
Dear all:

According to Scope item 33Q, I want to test Actual Costing Runs, but I cannot find the app - Edit Actual Costing Runs (CKMLCP) in My Public Cloud System. Even though I have added the business catalog 'SAP_FIN_BC_IA_AC_PER_ACT_PC '　according note 3408712.

Does anyone know where the problem lies?
Thank you and best regard!

Wang

entry.title: Re: 403 Forbidden Error on API-Proxy (BTP Integration Sui...
entry.content:
It looks indeed like the 403 is coming from the backend system. In that case, the ICM trace should show a few more details.

Also you need to be a bit careful: if there is a "System Certificate" defined in the Cloud Connector, then by default the SCC will use that for logging on to backend systems. That could then interfere with the Basic credentials contained in the request header!

There are two ways to fix this: either on backend side, you can configure the ICM listening port to not request a client certificate, then the SCC won't send his. (Can be defined in the profile parameter that sets up the port.) Or alternatively somewhere in the SCC settings there must be a parameter that tells the SCC not to use a client certificate for authentication. Oh, and I think in the definition of the "Service" in SICF, you can also specify, which authentication methods the Service takes into account, and in which order. So if you put "Basic" at the very top, it might also help.

entry.title: Re: Apply header-level freight split to line items based...
entry.content:
Dear @manish2k,

As suggested by @Nina_Kou, Change the Calculation type in the condition type to Fixed Amount (B), and to split the price based on the Line item.

Set the Alternative Base amount field in Pricing to any of the below
"1" - Volume = Splits the amount based on the Volume of each line item
"12" - Gross weight = Splits the amount based on the Gross weight
"13" - Net weight = Splits the amount based on the Net Weight

Note: Group condition is not required for this scenario remove the Group condition and Group routine in the condition type.

Hope this helps!!!

Best Regards,
Surya.

entry.title: Re: Actual Costing - Revaluation of Consumption in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Dear Alina,

Thanks for the document, 

Could you please touch upon the impact of 'revaluate material' option in ckmlcp?

If we want to correct the price of the material in MR21 (for inventory valuation) the difference should stay in P&L and should not again post to revaluation as (settlement) during month end actual costing exercise.

Regards

Rajan.

entry.title: Re: Purpose Codes in Payment Media in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Dear @merle2, 

it could also be in the mapping table of purpose codes where there are also fields for Sender Bank Country and Recipient Bank Country. Please make sure that the mapping reflects the bank countries in the given payment. If it does not help, feel free to raise an incident on the component FI-AP-AP-B1 and we will have a look at it.

Thanks and regards,

Ondrej

entry.title: Can't get the value from OData v4 entity set
entry.content:
I created a new user and retrieved data from entity set 'BANK'.  But the return value is empty even the entity set has value.

 

It can retrieved data from entity set 'ZCDS_ZTALCUSTOMER'

Another sap-all user can retrieve data from entity set 'BANK'.. 

 

So I doubt there is any authorization  is forgotten. Do you have any idea?

entry.title: Re: how to provision assignments with validity date from SAP IPS to AS ABAP system
entry.content: Submitted a ticket to SAP , and the response from the product support team is that if the file is not available in the default transformation, then it is not possible. Shocked to see this response. If this is the case then in default write transformation logic there is no valid from,SNC, valid to, and other su01 related fields as the BAPI_USER_CHANGE contains those fields if we include them it pushes the date. but for roles they haven't specified how the groups part is working if it comes under patch operation, then how to iterate the validity date for each user as it would be different.

We have seen that Personal Profile is now offered (containing User Info) with the release 1H 2025. it can also be relocated to position 1 in profile

entry.content: Hello were you able to find the solution?

entry.title: Re: Utilities and Tax Payments in Brazil
entry.content:
Dear Prabhjyot_Singh,

format supplement TAXUT is mandatory. It is hard-coded in our logic. I am not sure whether you are on S/4 HANA Public Cloud or on OnPremise, but on Public Cloud we deliver it as a pre-delivered content. FYI, refer also to docu on https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/c5e66d2df826499d9b8c76b8355601c3/a38903602bf649998dde58d389b07036.html where it is listed as a prerequisite.

Kind regards,

Ondrej

entry.title: Re: How to create an Authorization Group for: "Define allowlists for CDS view extraction"
entry.content: how to add to Transport Request?

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA 2023 Fully-Activated Appliance: Known Issues
entry.content: Oh, OK sorry, I was considering re-install to get maintenance on, and was wondering if I should just re-install, or go the maintenance way. So that's good to know ...

entry.title: Re: Sending order confirmation with attachments via email...
entry.content:
Dear @Omid_PS, 

Add the General terms and conditions and cancellation policy to any of the below and include the same in the Sales order confirmation forms and send it through mail.

1. Use the "Maintain Standard Texts" app and enter the required General terms and conditions and cancellation policy and using the Text determination in Sales order copy those to every sales order header text and bind these Header text into last page of the form and use the OPD to send the email.

2. Use the "Manage Footer" assign this in the determination (to company code or plant), as a master form and then use this in form and send through mail.

Hope this helps!!!

Best Regards,
Surya.

entry.title: Re: Personas EWM RF S/4HANA Public Cloud - custom input f...
entry.content:
Hi,

You have to use scripting to achieve desired behaviour.

onLoad screen event will not help you to set initial focus to custom field, as RFUI applications are different and navigation between screens will not guarantee that context is changed. So onLoad could fire on different screen and ignored on your.

It is better to have onAfterRefresh event script which will check if custom control exists on the screen and then sets focus.

if(session.idExists('wnd[0]/usr/subX:/SCWM/SAPLRF_INQUIRY_PM:0311/txtPersonas_175136377313095')) {
   session.findById('wnd[0]/usr/subX:/SCWM/SAPLRF_INQUIRY_PM:0311/txtPersonas_175136377313095').setFocus();
}

NOTE:
It is better to place this custom field inside the same simple container as the native input fields on the screen. If you will put custom control into the user area, then it will be visible on all other screens after navigation.

But, there is the big BUT. Any Scripting involvement will generate more requests in background, comparing to no script Flavor. This is needed to support scripting functionality and get the latest controls state. However this also will impact performance, especially on mobile devices.
Let's say you scan value without Flavor. This will generate one screen update HTTP request 300ms. 
Compare to Flavor with scripting. You are setting Focus, Scanned value is added to custom field and you add some logic to parse this scanned value and after that script will set value of native control. All this will generate 3-5 HTTP requests. 
So you will have to wait ~1-1.5 seconds.

The recommendation from SAP Screen Personas is to use Personas and ABAP together:

  • Use Flavor for visual customisations only (exclude screen events at least)
  • Use ABAP coding to process/parse any data (like split scanned value and fill several fields)

It is VERY IMPORTANT to create initial version of Flavor and test it as soon as possible on real device and real scenario. 

 

As of TAB sequence, input fields have "tabToElement" property (available through Advanced Property Editor). There you can put ID of next control with fill be focused after TAB key.

For example, if you have two inputs: A, B. TAB sequence is A -> B -> some other control.
Set "tabToElement=[control_ID_of_A]" to B input control. The focus will be cycling A -> B-> A-> B-> ...

 

Best Regards!

entry.title: UD return to vendor should reset WM delivery from C to A
entry.content:
Hello,

My client is using WM for warehouse management. When they receive PO the delivery is created and the materials are distributed base on that to warehouse. But we are also using QM.  So we have scenario when the materials are received and posted to stock with delivery and delivery is in status C. Now we do UD but the parts are wrong and decision is made return to vendor, automatic return document and movement 122 is done.

The problem is when we receive the parts back, we can't do GR for the PO any more as delivery is in status C. Is there any custom solution to get workaround not to increase PO quantity only reopen delivery and do GR once again to the same delivery?

entry.title: Re: SAP S4/HANA Embedded EWM Configuration - Blog 1 - step by step Guide - Organization structure
entry.content: Doing this since 2001. Business articulation, illustrates misalignment, it may not take the latest technology to fix the problem, it may take a change in management to remedy the problem, it's become evident that there is no alternative to digitalisation. Once again, computer are like drugs they can either cure you or kill you. It's  the health practitioner that diagnosis the rca, i.e., identifies the correct drug and regimen thereafter to cure the patient.. It's  not the latest drug that gets the accolade but the health practitioner for diagnosing the root cause.  We did something similar in 1982 with a Talbot system from the UK, a vehicle tracking system that used coloured Hollerith punched cards to track the vehicles progress and also parts and accessories required to replenish the bins at overnight.  So not to stop the production line.  First, build to order cars ever.  Stock keeping levels drop from 30M per month to optimal stock level of 17m per month. Great insight.

entry.title: Re: SAP API Management - How to create a Cloud Integration Flow API Provider
entry.content: Excellent! The explaination is very clear, it works

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for June – SAP CAP ❄️ 🏔️
entry.content: @M-K Please check under the tab Personal -> Personal Information as seen in your screenshot. You should get an option to set your display name there.

entry.title: Delete provisoned Rolles
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

we are using SAP IPS to provision groups from IAS to Enable Now. In Enable Now, the provisioned IAS groups are automatically converted into roles.

  1. How can I delete these provisioned roles in Enable Now?
    They were automatically created through provisioning from IAS and we want to remove some of them.

  2. Is it possible to overwrite manually created roles with provisioned ones?
    For example, we have a group called "Admins" in IAS and a manually created role with the same name "Admins" already exists in Enable Now.
    Can the provisioning be configured in such a way that it does not create a new role in Enable Now but instead updates or takes the existing one?

Thank you in advance

Best regards

entry.title: Manage Customer returns - greyed out refund control
entry.content:
In the app "Manage Customer Returns - Version 2", Once you choose the refund control "Decide later" in order to first execute the Returns delivery and inspection process, you can not go back to modify the refund control option from "Decide later" to any other option like "Create credit memo request / Create credit memo / No Refund".

Is there a reason why it is greyed out and not allowing to be "Decided later" if that makes more sense from a process perspective?

And is there anyway to overrun it and choose a specific refund control like the creating of a credit memo request or a credit memo.

Thank you all in advance for any answers or ideas!

entry.title: Re: PERIOD ERROR
entry.content: The said company code has already been initialised.  Check in MMRV which period is showing

entry.title: Re: 🚀 What’s New in Cloud Control Center PL21 – Smarter, Safer, and More in Control
entry.content: Thanks for the update and blog post,  Guy. One question though: does the initialization of the analytics also keep the UDF configuration of the original database?

entry.title: PLM Tech Uncovered: SAP Integrated Product Development Update 06/2025
entry.content:
New features alert for SAP Integrated Product Development!

June 2025 brings the following highlights for your business:

Formulation

  • User experience and calculation improvements
    Administrators can now define which component types are allowed for balancing items in formulas using the Configure Component Types for Formulation app. These component types can't be excluded from calculations.
  • New composition item group filter
    A new composition item group filter is now available for quantitative/qualitative calculations on the Calculations tab and in the Transfer Calculation Results to Primary Output Specification dialog box.
  • New columns and navigation options in the formula
    A new Item No. column is now available in the formula layout. It shows the position of each formula item in the formula structure. Users can also add a new Item Type column through personalization settings. This column will show the specific formula item type for each displayed item. Additionally, drag-and-drop is now supported for multiple formula items at once.
  • New Compare Recipes app
    A new Compare Recipes app is now available. The app lets product developers identify and analyze differences and similarities between multiple recipe formulas.

Collaboration

  • Superviewer user group is added to provide read access to all collaborations
    As an administrator, when you create the new user group DL_SUPER_VIEWER_GROUP user group in the SAP BTP cockpit within your subaccount, you assign the email IDs to this user group, in order to provide view-only access to all collaborations.

Product Data Integration

  • Integration of the Collaboration Capability
    Design Item Explorer app and API both allow assigning and unassigning collaborations to/from design items.
  • Versioning via release tags
    Design Item Explorer now supports versioning via release tags fully.
    Users can now switch the displayed release tags and iterations of a design item and create a new release tag (e.g. to continue design work on a new version of a released design item)

Product Handover

  • View sub-tasks for each Task Action in the Task Monitoring for Handovers app
    You can now view all the sub-tasks with time lines for each of the Task Actions when handover is created from EBOM to MBOM and SAP Integrated Product Development recipe formulation to MBOM. You can view the timelines on the task object page for the sub-tasks.
  • Monitor task status for the Hand Over Recipe Formulation to Bills of Material (BOM) app
    You can now monitor the progress of the action performed during the handover of recipe to Manufacturing Bills of Material (MBOM) in the Task Monitoring for Handovers app. The task actions include initiating a handover, incorporating changes, and publishing an MBOM.
  • Search and initiate handovers for configurable BOMs associated with global object dependencies
    You can now search and initiate handovers for BOMs associated with object dependencies in the Hand Over Engineering Bills of Material (EBOM) app.
  • Planning components with object dependencies
    You can now plan components with object dependencies from the source panel to the target panel on the planner page.

Specification Management

  • Authorization contexts for specifications moved to Common Functions
    The Manage Authorization Contexts app, where administrators can maintain access restrictions for users or teams of Specification Management, has been moved to the Common Functions SaaS application.
  • Specification workflow integration and manual triggering
    Product Developers can trigger workflows manually. If a specification is created with a specification type that has a workflow assignment, the Next Steps button appears in the Manage Specifications app.
  • Property IDs hidden in Manage Specifications
    Property names are now displayed in the property tree without their property IDs.
  • Usability enhancements for specification inheritance
    The target specifications of a source specification are displayed and you can navigate between a source specification and its target specifications.

Engineering

  • Major and minor versions
    You now have the option to save changes as a minor version when updating a requirement model using the "Save with Comments" function. This allows for smaller and less disruptive updates to be recorded, without triggering the creation of a new major version. When you publish a requirement, a new major version is generated.

To stay informed until the next update you can:

🖊 Subscribe to the What’s New Viewer on SAP Integrated Product Development Help Portal 
🔗 Join our LinkedIn group SAP Solutions for PLM 
🌐 Visit us at www.sap.com/IPD

entry.title: Re: PERIOD ERROR
entry.content:
12/2025 is showing so that I'm not able to PGI.

 

Am I able to PGI if it is in 07/2025? how to change that?

pls clarify @Lakshmipathi

entry.title: Re: Reimagining SAP MDG Field Validations Low-Code Approach | UI Field Control Made Dynamic Yet Simp
entry.content: Nice Blog 🙂 @Nimii  with clear explanation

entry.title: landscape portal not visible even after subscribing booster in SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hi All ,

We have subscribed to SAP BTP ABAP environment but it is not started as it seems to be hibernated .  SAP suggests to use landscape portal . To start it we need Landscape portal service. We have added it through booster and completed the configuration . But its not visible anywhere. As per the steps 3 subaccounts were supposed to be created but we cannot see anything.

Region selected was US-east when completing the steps,

Please let me know what is missing here . Should we add the entitlement manually and do we need to select subaccount in region europe?

Please suggest . We are stuck and we cannot access SAP btp fiori launchpad from ABAP environment as its hibernated and we need to log in landscape portal to start it.

Regards

Sweta

entry.title: Issue with setting up connectivity from BTP to Converged Cloud
entry.content:
We are trying to run a script as a Cloud Foundry function on BTP to pull data from Converged Cloud (asset data) , and we are failing to connect despite having the cloud connector set up (On the cloud connector page, it says "available" for the converged cloud endpoints that we set up). Also, the code runs locally just fine.

Did someone else face a similar challenge, or do you have any idea who we could work with to troubleshoot this?

Thanks in advance for your time and help!

Petr

The error:

Authentication error at https://identity-3.ap-ae-1.cloud.sap/v3/auth/tokens: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='identity-3.ap-ae-1.cloud.sap', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /v3/auth/tokens (Caused by NameResolutionError("<urllib3.connection.HTTPSConnection object at 0x10689f0e0>: Failed to resolve 'identity-3.ap-ae-1.cloud.sap' ([Errno 8] nodename nor servname provided, or not known)"))

entry.title: Re: Reimagining SAP MDG Field Validations Low-Code Approach | UI Field Control Made Dynamic Yet Simp
entry.content: @Nimii A Question, how to make USMD_REASON Field Mandatory which related to change request, which is present in General Data UIBB Section ?

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM Configuration in S4HANA system (on-premise)
entry.content:
Hi Venu,

Thank you for sharing the blog.

@Venuobbireddy816 The blog works fine with S4 RISE & ECS hosted systems but not with on-prem. 

When is comes to S4 On-prem to Cloud ALM I'm stuck with similar issue. (( Direct connect to *****-ltd-cloudalm.authentication.<region>.hana.ondemand.com:443 failed: NIECONN_REFUSED(-10) )) Any advise would be helpful. 

Tried 3106170 - SAP Cloud ALM: Register error while adding private Cloud hosted ABAP systems (S/4HANA, Business Suite, SAP NetWeaver Application Server for ABAP systems) - SAP for Me

Curl gives HTTP/1.1 200 OK for both the URLS ((  API URL & OAuth URL ))


@HemantVaswani6 for the SSL error looks like source and target aren't able to establish the connection. Try this Setup STRUST  (( https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/setup-managed-services/setup-abap/setup-strust.html )) if this helps.

Kind Regards
Sunam

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for June – SAP CAP ❄️ 🏔️

entry.title: On Create Initial Balance, an error message "Memo record type is invalid" occurs.
entry.content:
1. Memo record type invalid error in the App Create Initial Balance and Balance Adjustment with Reference.
  2. F4 help for Memo Record Type in the App Create Initial Balance and Balance Adjustment with Reference displays a message "No Value Found".

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: On Create Initial Balance, an error message "Memo rec...
entry.content:
The root cause of this issue is that not all memo record types are configured for use in creating Initial Balance Uploads. To address this, please follow the steps below:  

1. Navigate to the configuration activity "Define Memo Record Types".  
2. Either define a custom memo type or use an existing standard memo type.  
3. In the memo record category, select "IBU (Initial Balance Flows)".  
4. Save the configuration changes.  

Once these steps are completed, the IBU memo type will appear in the F4 value help for the "Memo Record Type" field within the app Initial Balance and Balance Adjustment with Reference.

entry.title: How to handle the life-cycle of flows for different environment?
entry.content:
Hi,

I have created a flow in Sap Build Process Automation.
I have created three environments in Sap Build Process Automation , dev, test and production.
I deployed my "1.0" flow to the test, tested it and then deployed to the production environment.

Now I need to work on new features for the flow, so I make changes, release a new version for the flow with the changes, make some more changes and deploy to the test environment to test it etc...
All the while the 1.0 is deployed on the prod environment.
Lets say that now im told there is a bug in the 1.0 flow.

How do i now, after creating many new versions based on the 1.0 flow, go back to working on the 1.0 without deleting all the changes i have added for the new features?

What is the best practice way to handle such a situation?

entry.title: Internal Reconciliation for BP with Both Customer and Supplier Roles
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

When a Business Partner (BP) is maintained as both a customer and a supplier (vendor) in SAP S/4HANA, we would like to perform internal reconciliation or netting at month-end—not just for payments, but for all open items. The objective is to offset payables against receivables so that the net balance is reflected on a single side (Asset or liability) in the Trial Balance.

Could you please guide us on how to carry out this internal reconciliation process? Any best practices, TCodes, or configuration recommendations would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your support!

Best Regards,

silambarasan R

entry.title: Re: 🚀 What’s New in Cloud Control Center PL21 – Smarter, Safer, and More in Control
entry.content:
Hi @JoergAldinger 
Thanks for this important question. With PL21 we fulfilled the initial gap by automating the initialization on  <target> Tenant after Upgrade or Duplication. As a second step, we plan to further minimize the post upgrade efforts for HANA tenants (due to the nature upgrades in CCC which is not an "in place" upgrade)  by introducing an export/import option for UDF Configuration applied in Analytics Calculation views, so...this is indeed in the pipeline. 

Best Rgs
Guy

entry.title: Re: Print HU label at Product WT confirmation in S/4HANA...
entry.content:
Hi @Jeroen_Bosman , we are still talking about printing for a warehouse order / warehouse task correct?

In your example, since you want to print at the destination bin only, can't you just include the storage type or activity area into your output parameter determination since they are already available as standard fields?

Otherwise, if you have a very custom requirement on when to print, you can also define a custom field to determine the printout and use BAdI /SCWM/EX4_OM_OPD_EXTEND_WO to determine the value of that custom field.

Best regards,
Christian

entry.title: Re: SAP Dynamic Forms Custom Measurement Point Control No...
entry.content:
Hi I am facing the same problem.

Waiting for dynamic forms 2505 release.

Also, I do see that the field "reading" is not visible using the industry control "Measurement Point".

So, at this moment whatever you enter sap backend interprets that reading as Note!

entry.title: When entering stock and non stock item in order cost is getting updated in one category YB2
entry.content:
When entering stock and non stock item in order cost is getting updated in one category YB2

We are trying to maintain materials for consumption (stock item) and procurement (non stock item) in maintenance order but after entering the cost is getting updated in one category only YB2. Ideally it should go to YB2 for stock items and YB3 for non stock items. 

Material type used is ERSA

Please can anyone suggest what is missing in material or configuration

entry.title: Re: Manage Customer returns - greyed out refund control
entry.content: Not sure whether you already gone through this  Help  documentation where it does not explain your query but documented on credit note.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Experience Sharing: How to Batch Modify the Assignees of Calendar Processes
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud Experience Sharing: How to Batch Modify the Assignees of Calendar Processes

I. Introduction

In today's digital - driven era where businesses strive for excellence in operations, SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)  serves as a powerful all - in - one tool. It boosts enterprises' decision - making efficiency and managerial order, handling data visualization, predictive analysis, planning, and collaboration. It draws a grand blueprint for intelligent business management.

Yet, as companies expand, refine their business processes, and deepen teamwork, the demands on SAC's features are also increasing. Focusing on its calendar function, although it performs well in event scheduling and process tracking, its filtering mechanism is somewhat limited. When filtering for event - related individuals, it only allows filtering by "My Roles", which can't satisfy the diverse and complex management needs of enterprises.

Consider this scenario: When employee A takes a leave, event 1's process owner B wants to quickly identify the incomplete sub - tasks under this event assigned to A and reallocate human resources. However, due to the limitations of the filtering function, the operation will encounter some difficulties. When employee A leaves the company, the calendar administrator has to take on the onerous task of searching for A in massive amounts of calendar data and replacing them one by one. Facing the lengthy and complicated operational process, the efficiency is reduced. To address this pain point, I have developed a story application using a calendar API script. In this article, I will introduce it to you in detail.

II. How to Use the "Calendar Process Assignee Batch Modification" Application

Before using this story app, you can find its usage instructions on the story page.

1. To perform this action, you need the following permissions:

  • Read access to the Analytical Model.
  • Manage access to Public Files.
  • Read access to Private Files.

2. Please follow these steps to get the event ID of the parent calendar process:

  • Click “Calendar” in the navigation bar and select the event you want to edit.

  • When the event page opens, copy the part after “eventId=” in the browser's address bar URL to get the event ID.

Alternatively:

  • After selecting the event, click "Details" in the top - right corner. Then, in the top - right corner of the pop - up menu, click "More", and then click "Event Settings".

  • If the “Allow External API Access” option isn't checked, please check it.

  • Then click “Show Details” to view and copy the event ID.

3. Paste the event ID into the text box.

4. Use the dropdown menu to select the assignee you want to replace.

5. Use the dropdown menu again to select the new assignee you want to add.

6. Click the "Confirm and Replace" button.

  • For events where replacement is successful, a green window will pop up to indicate completion.
  • Once all tasks are finished, a gray window will appear with the message "Finished processing all processes and tasks" to indicate that everything is complete.

 

  • If there are events that failed to replace during the entire process replacement, the system will prompt the number of events that were not successfully replaced, as well as the corresponding event IDs and names.

III. Notes

1. For Process Events Only

  • Can only be used on a parent process, other types of calendar events are not supported. If you input the event ID of a non - process event, such as a General task, the system will display a prompt: "Only events with the event type of'Process' can be modified. Please enter the Event ID of an event with the event type of 'Process'."

2. Restrictions on Task Status

  • Cannot replace assignees of tasks  with statuses of "Canceled, Failed, Successful, or Partially Successful".   

3. Restrictions on Task Type

  • Cannot replace assignees of Data Locking Tasks, Data Action Tasks, Multi Action Tasks, Input Tasks, or Publications.

IV. Code Implementation

Next, I'll introduce the main implementation logic. Calendar offers a wide range of integration APIs, and the above functions can be realised by using relevant APIs. The major logic steps are as follows:

1. Variable Initialisation and Interface Settings

Before implementing this function, you need to configure “Calendar Integration” in the story app and initialise the variables. Here are some key variables:

RemovedAssignee // The assignee to be replaced
AddedAssignee // The new assignee to be added
calendarEventId // The event ID of the relevant calendar
removeAssigneeFilterSelection // Script variable for the dropdown to remove an assignee
addAssigneeFilterSelection // Script variable for the dropdown to add an assignee
count // Counter for events that failed to replace
arrayFailedEvent // Collection of failed event IDs
arrayFailedEventName // Collection of failed event names

You can also preset and configure the display of loading states and the type of system message prompts.

2. Get the Event Object and Perform Validation

First, get the corresponding event object task based on  calendarEventId and check whether it exists.Then check whether the event type is "Process". If not, notify the user that this feature only supports "Process" - type events and stop further execution.

var task = calIntegration1.getCalendarEventById(calendarEventId); 
if(task === undefined){ 
               Application.showMessage( 
               ApplicationMessageType.Warning, 
               "The event ID you entered is incorrect. Please verify and re-enter it." 
); 
               // used to manage the loading state.
               Application.hideBusyIndicator(); 
               return; 
} 
// Add a check to determine whether the event type of the user - entered event is a process or something else.
if(task.getType() !== CalendarTaskType.Process ){ 
               Application.showMessage( 
               ApplicationMessageType.Warning, 
               "Only events with the event type of \"Process\" can be modified. Please enter the Event ID of an event with the event type of \"Process.\"" 
); 
               // used to manage the loading state.
               Application.hideBusyIndicator(); 
               return; 
}

3. Process the Root Process Task

Cast the event object to the CalendarProcess type.

Perform the removeAssignee operation on the root process.

  • If removal succeeds, add the new assignee using addAssignee.
  • Output corresponding messages based on operation success or failure.
  • If it fails, log the failure info and add it to the failure array.
var task_process = cast(Type.CalendarProcess,task);
// Create and initialize a queue (using an array to simulate a queue).
var eventQueue = ArrayUtils.create(Type.CalendarProcess); 
// Enqueue the root process.
eventQueue.push(task_process); 
var assigneeRemoved_root = task_process.removeAssignee(RemovedAssignee); 
// Used to control the loading status. 
Application.hideBusyIndicator(); 
// Check if removing the assignee from the process root was successful.
 if(assigneeRemoved_root) { 
     Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Success, "Assignee has been successfully removeded to Calendar Event " );    
      // Add the new assignee to the root process only after the assignee deletion is confirmed to be successful.
      var assigneeAdded_root = task_process.addAssignee(AddedAssignee);    
      // Check if adding the assignee to the root process was successful.
      if(assigneeAdded_root) {  
            Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Success, "Assignee has been successfully addded to Calendar Event " );
      }  
      else {  
            Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Error, "Assignee has failed to be added to Calendar Event ");
      } 
 }  
    else {  
        Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Error, "Assignee has failed to be removed to Calendar Event ");
        count++;
       arrayFailedEventName.push(calIntegration1.getCalendarEventById(calendarEventId).getName()); 
        arrayFailedEvent.push(calendarEventId);
    }

4. Traverse All Subprocesses and Subtasks Using BFS

Perform a breadth - first traversal of all sub - events in the eventQueue.

For each sub - event:

  • Get its object.
  • If it's a process type, enqueue it to traverse its sub - items.
  • For each sub - item, try to remove the original assignee. If successful, add the new assignee.
  • Record logs and info regardless of success or failure. Add failed ones to the failure array.
// Process all tasks via breadth - first traversal.    
while (eventQueue.length > 0) { 
    // Remove the current process from the front of the queue.    
    var currentProcess = eventQueue.shift(); 
    // Retrieve all sub - items (sub - processes or sub - tasks) of the current process.
    var childrenIds = currentProcess.getChildren(); 
// Traverse all sub - items.
    for (var i = 0; i < childrenIds.length; i++) { 
        var childEvent = calIntegration1.getCalendarEventById(childrenIds[i]);               
        // If a sub - item is a sub - process, add it to the queue for further processing of its own sub - items.    
        if (childEvent.getType() === CalendarTaskType.Process) { 
            var childProcess = cast(Type.CalendarProcess, childEvent); 
            eventQueue.push(childProcess); // Add the sub - process to the queue.  
        }          
        // Perform the removal operation on the current sub - item (whether it's a process or a task).   
        var assigneeRemoved = childEvent.removeAssignee(RemovedAssignee); 
        // Process the deletion - operation result.  
        if (assigneeRemoved) { 
            Application.showMessage( 
                ApplicationMessageType.Success, 
                "Assignee removed from: " + childEvent.getId() 
            ); 
           var assigneeAdded = childEvent.addAssignee(AddedAssignee); 
                 // Process the addition - operation result.     
                if (assigneeAdded) { 
                              Application.showMessage( 
                                  ApplicationMessageType.Success, 
                                  "Assignee added to: " + childEvent.getId() 
                     );                                                                     } 

               else { 

                              Application.showMessage( 
                                  ApplicationMessageType.Error, 
                                  "Failed to add to: " + childEvent.getId() 
                              ); 
               }                              
        }  
       else { 
                        count++; 
                        arrayFailedEvent.push(childEvent.getId());                                        arrayFailedEventName.push(calIntegration1.getCalendarEventById(childEvent.getId()).getName());  
                        Application.showMessage( 
                            ApplicationMessageType.Error, 
                            "Failed to remove from: " + childEvent.getId() 
                        ); 
       }                         
    } 
}

5. Output Error Messages and Result Prompts

  • If there are failed events, display the number of failures.
  • For each failed event, show its name and ID.
  • Finally, display a " Finished processing all processes and tasks " message.
var count_string = ConvertUtils.numberToString(count);
// Check if any events failed to replace. If so, output the number of these events.
if(count !== 0){
               Application.showMessage(
               ApplicationMessageType.Warning,
               "You have \"" + count_string + "\" events where the assignee failed to be replaced successfully." 
               );
} 
for (var j = 0; j < count; j++) {
               warningMessage = warningMessage + "The assignee of event  \"" + arrayFailedEventName[j]  + "\" failed to be replaced successfully.\n";
               warningMessage = warningMessage + " the event ID: " + arrayFailedEvent[j] + "\n\n";
}
if (warningMessage) {
               Application.showMessage(
               ApplicationMessageType.Warning,
               warningMessage
               );
}
// Task execution completed.  
Application.showMessage(
              ApplicationMessageType.Info,
             "Finished processing all processes and tasks"
);

V. Summary

This article mainly shares my experience in developing the story application for "Batch Modifying Calendar Process Assignees". If you have encountered similar problems, I hope it can provide you with some inspiration.

entry.title: Re: Mandatory document attachment tab in Manage Certifica...
entry.content: Can you suggest ??

entry.title: Re: How to link screen-painter element to custom table
entry.content:
Declare a variable in the program and in the PAI section of the screen logic, you set that variable to the value of input field DATE_AGO. 

Example PAI logic:

PROCESS AFTER INPUT.
  MODULE exit AT EXIT-COMMAND.
  FIELD:
         DATE_AGO.
  MODULE get_data.

......

MODULE get_data OUTPUT.
  lv_date_ago = date_ago.
ENDMODULE.

entry.title: Re: Cannot set breakpoint because 'main program must be generated '
entry.content: For me this was due to missing method implementation in quality system. Please check if all required TR's moved.

entry.title: Re: New features in SAP Warehouse Operator Version 2.5
entry.content:
Hello @chrisjsosa , 

there is no documentation available for the setup of the app for the Private Cloud. The current setup guide is only available for the Public Cloud. Private Cloud support is also not part of the roadmap. Technically it's possible since the 2023 release for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition. Prerequisites are SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) with SAP Mobile Services and the configuration of the Cloud Connector to make the connection between your SAP BTP applications and on-premises systems.

Regards, 
Christian

entry.title: Re: ABAP code to find MTTR / MTBR
entry.content: I was just grinding through the whole Program to find mttr logic😂. Just understand the logic through examples/scenarios, then go for code.

entry.title: We cannot settle the AUC values to GL account after upgrade.
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have upgraded from 1809 to 2023 version.
 
Case 1: When we are trying to execute AIAB with the following expenditure GL account's 63800180 / 63800190 then we are getting the error ' Receiver categ. KS is not allowed by settlement prof. AI for this sender.' Before the upgrade user was able to execute AIAB using this GL - 63800180 / 63800190. Please help as to how user can use these two accounts for AIAB?
 
Case 2: Now when user is executing AIAB using GL account 23201100 it is successfully saving. But when we execute AIBU for this GL 23201100 for previous year asset to current year then it is giving the error - 'Settlement of Old asset data to receiver type GL Not allowed'. For this also before the upgrade user was able to execute this successfully. How can we avoid this error?
 
We have also replicated this error in QAS system, please find the attached for documentation.

Regards,

Naveen

entry.title: sysOverallcustom1 and sysOverallcustom2 in Succession org chart
entry.content:
Hi,

We have configured sysOverallcustom1 and sysOverallcustom2 because we want 2 extra ratings besides the regular Performance, Potential, Competency and Objectives. 

We would like to do an initial import on these 2 trend elements, and them to fill them in People Profile.

I added the blocks in People Profile but I see it is picking up all the overall ratings available in the system (Performance, Potential, Competency).  Anyone knows how do I mention what the rating scale should be for these 2 custom ratings and how do I stop them from picking up other ratings?

 

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: getting csv file from AL11 into internal table,append...
entry.content:
You can try the SPLIT command.  For example:

 

SPLIT lv_new AT cl_abap_char_utilities=>newline INTO TABLE lt_new.

entry.title: Survey Management Capability in SAP CX Version2
entry.content:
We procured SAP CX Version 2.

In SAP CX V2, we have CDC, CDP, Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, and SAP Emarsys.

Is it possible to manage Surveys (internal as well as external) through SAP CX Version 2?

I read this Surveys | SAP Help Portal here, where it is clearly mentioned Surveys are categorized into internal and external surveys. 

Please guide and assist.

entry.title: Re: Eclipse "Install in this IDE" (context of keeping ADT...
entry.content: I did the exact same "Install in this IDE" on another laptop, and nothing changed, it's still apparently 2025-03 but with workspace migrated to 2025-06 and ADT 3.50. I did not delete this installation, so I can look at the installation history or whatever you want in case you have any question.

entry.title: Re: SAP Query - Issue with missing fields on Selection Sc...
entry.content: I did review this note and followed the steps, but fields are still missing from selection screen. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: 27. SAP Stammtisch OST in St.Gallen am 1.Juli 2025

entry.title: Re: Survey Management Capability in SAP CX Version2

Hello @muhammad_asad_qureshi 

Please follow the latest business documentation on Configure Survey in SAP Slaes Cloud V2 which will suffice your need of exposing surveys externally. Feel free to reply for further queries.
If it helped, mark thread as accepted solution.
Thanks & Regards,
Kunal Bansal, SAP CX SME
https://www.linkedin.com/in/thekunalbansal

entry.title: Re: How to configure presentation device for EWM
entry.content:
Hello,

Please have a look at /SCWM/PRDVC.

Here you can create your required device

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for June – SAP CAP ❄️ 🏔️
entry.content:
Hey @M-K @ajay_soreng @mts_at_nn @ i found user id in the changed url by following below steps

1. Click top icon my profile.

2. Click on my content as shown in screenshot.

3. Check the url at the top in end of that url you will find your user id.

Example for me : https://community.sap.com/t5/user/viewprofilepage/user-id/1398756

4. Hence my user id 1398756

 

For changing display name can click on edit icon as shown below:

entry.title: Re: Deployment Error In SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content: Hello.. What solution SAP has provided? How did you/SAP solved the issue?

entry.title: Need help with SAP MAG - XSLT mapping context
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing an issue while populating target field in MAG. E.g.  I have kept one-to-one mapping for //E1EDl24/POSNR --> //HL-I/LIN/350 . When E1EDL4 appearing only one time then working fine if E1EDL24 appearing multiple times then //HL-I/LIN/350 populating with all the source values (POSNR). Please suggest how to fix this.

Target XML like below , when it should contain 000010 in the first occurrence and 000020 in the 2nd.

entry.title: Re: sapgui 800 Patch 6 - error 12007 despite xml file is accessible
entry.content: Hello Baurycza, would you mind attaching a screenshot of the issue.

entry.title: Rise into the Future – Simplify your path to SAP Cloud ERP
entry.content:
Businesses today recognize the need to modernize their operations, but the path to digital transformation is often fraught with uncertainty. How can organizations move to cloud ERP efficiently, securely, and with the right support? The answer lies in a structured, proven methodology—like that offered by RISE with SAP—which guides companies every step of the way, from initial analysis to full cloud adoption and beyond.

That will be the major topic of discussion in an upcoming Webinar - Rise into the Future: Modernize your ERP using RISE with SAP Methodology on July 24th.

Why Move to Cloud ERP?

Cloud ERP is no longer just an option; it’s a strategic imperative. As industries become increasingly digital, companies that remain tethered to on-premises systems risk falling behind. Cloud ERP solutions, such as those provided by SAP, unlock new potential by enabling faster innovation, greater agility, and market differentiation:

  • Faster Innovation: Cloud ERP platforms deliver continuous updates and access to cutting-edge technologies, including artificial intelligence (AI) and robotic process automation (RPA). This means businesses can adopt new features and capabilities as soon as they become available, keeping pace with market demands.
  • Enhanced Agility: A cloud-based core allows organizations to be more responsive to change. Processes can be automated, inefficiencies reduced, and new business models introduced with minimal disruption.
  • Improved Resilience and Compliance: Cloud ERP provides advanced security, robust compliance frameworks, and business continuity features that are challenging to match on-premises. This ensures that data is protected, and business operations remain uninterrupted even in the face of disruptions.
  • Cost Reduction and Operational Efficiency: By consolidating software, infrastructure, and services into a single package, companies can reduce IT complexity and total cost of ownership. Automation and analytics further optimize business processes, leading to cost savings and improved productivity.

The RISE with SAP Methodology: A Guided Transformation

RISE with SAP is a comprehensive, guided journey designed to help organizations modernize their SAP landscapes and unlock the full value of cloud ERP. Here’s how it works:

  • Standardized Framework: The methodology begins with a detailed analysis of existing processes, identifying areas for improvement to support the business strategy and defining a tailored transformation roadmap utilizing best business practice.
  • Clean Core Principles: RISE with SAP emphasizes maintaining a “clean core,” enabling organizations to extend and personalize their ERP without complicating core operations. This approach protects system integrity while allowing for high-value customizations.
  • Integrated Toolchain: Accelerated transformation with high quality outcomes through the intelligent features of the tools and the consistent integrated data usage.
  • Expert Guidance and Support: Throughout the transformation, SAP provides expert advice, managed services, and ongoing support. This means companies can focus on strategic initiatives rather than day-to-day IT management.

Ready to rise into the future? Join the conversation with SAP experts and discover how your organization can make the move to cloud ERP smarter and more successful

Click here to register and hear experts walk you through:

  • How to define a transformation road map using SAP Activate roadmap templates – tailored to your system landscape and goals.
  • How SAP’s toolchain enhances project management by streamlining task execution for process management and optimizing application lifecycle management. It also supports test automation and boosts user adoption.
  • How Joule accelerates key transformation steps, further facilitating efficient operations.
  • How you standardize your processes using clean core principles to reduce complexity and prepare your ERP for continuous innovation.

entry.title: Re: Couldn't import object. Parent resource does not exis...
entry.content:
Hi @PS_1978,

I saw this error message in the past when the folder, where the object I want to transport is located, is missing in the target tenant.
I was able to solve this issue by also importing the folder(s).

I hope this helps
Best regards
Susanne

entry.title: CAP Action Not Triggered in SAP Build Process Automation Workflow Form
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m working with SAP Build Process Automation and running into an issue where a CAP function is not being triggered from within a workflow form.

The action is defined as follows in CAP CDS, Cloud foundry runtime:

function functionQueryLandscapeObjects(
    search: Integer
) returns array of UILandscapeObjects;

In SAP Build Apps, I’ve added the service via the Action Editor and tested it there — everything works as expected. When I provide a valid search parameter (Business Partner ID), I get the correct data back.

However, in the actual workflow form, the function doesn’t seem to be triggered at all. The setup is like this:

  • The user selects a customer from a dropdown.
  • Based on the selected customer, the functionQueryLandscapeObjects action should be triggered to load related landscapes/systems.
  • The search parameter is properly set up to reference the selected Business Partner ID.
  • But: when the landscape dropdown should be populated, nothing happens. No data is shown.
  • Also, the CAP app doesn’t log anything in Cloud Foundry, which suggests the action isn’t called at all.

I’ve double-checked the destination, the bindings, and the input logic, and everything seems fine.

Has anyone experienced something similar?
Do I need to explicitly wire the action to a form event in Build Process Automation? Or is there a limitation with calling parameterized actions from forms?

Any help or examples would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance,

entry.title: SAP Quality Management process overview | SAP QM process overview
entry.content:
In Quality management there are three major branches first is Quality planning, second is Quality inspection and third is Quality notification.

When we talk about Quality Planning, it involves maintaining master data to define which materials need to be inspected and the parameters on which they should be evaluated before being released for further processing.

In Quality Inspection, there are seventeen standard processes through which materials are inspected to ensure compliance with defined quality standards.

As for Quality Notification, there are three major types, each used to raise notifications aimed at driving quality improvements within the organization.

In Quality management there are three major branches first is Quality planning, second is Quality inspection and third is Quality notification.

When we talk about Quality Planning, it involves maintaining master data to define which materials need to be inspected and the parameters on which they should be evaluated before being released for further processing.

In Quality Inspection, there are seventeen standard processes through which materials are inspected to ensure compliance with defined quality standards.

As for Quality Notification, there are three major types, each used to raise notifications aimed at driving quality improvements within the organization.

Here is detail of Quality inspection types:

01 – GR Inspection for Purchase Order
This inspection type is used for incoming inspection of goods received from external vendors against purchase order. It helps ensure that vendor-delivered materials meet the required quality standards before being posted to stock.

02 – GI inspection for Process order/Production Order
Inspection type 02 is used for goods issue inspections, specifically when materials are issued from a warehouse to production against production order/ Process order. It's important to note that inspection type 02 lots are generally stock-irrelevant meaning they do not directly affect the stock quantity or posting, just record results against lot and take UD.

03 – In-Process Inspection
This type enables quality checks during the actual production process. It’s typically used at operation-level inspection points (e.g., during mixing, welding, painting, assembling), allowing early detection of issues during manufacturing. it is also stock-irrelevant.

04 – Final Inspection from Process order/Production Order
This inspection occurs after production is completed but before the product is moved into finished goods stock or delivered. It ensures that the final product is compliant with specifications.

05 – GR for Other Goods Movements
Inspection type 05 is used to other goods receipts like incoming inspection of goods received from external vendors without purchase order reference or receiving market return/ sales return. Inspection type 02 lots are generally stock-relevant meaning you can hold or post stock through inspection lot.
06 – Return from Customers

While this is the standard purpose, you’ve indicated it’s used for sales return inspection in your business. This means when customers return goods, an inspection lot is triggered to assess the quality of the returned items. Inspection type 06 lots are generally stock-irrelevant meaning you can't hold or post stock through inspection lot.

07 – Vendor Audit
You’ve identified this as being used for audit inspections. This custom use allows companies to trigger inspection lots specifically for internal audits or external regulatory reviews.

08 – Stock Transfer
This inspection type is used for inspection after stock transfer from one plant to another. Inspection type 08 lots are generally stock-relevant meaning you can hold or post stock through inspection lot.

09 – Recurring Inspection
This supports scheduled, time-based inspections for stock already in inventory. It’s useful for materials with expiry dates or long-term storage, ensuring product integrity over time.

10 – Delivery inspection
Before shipping goods to customers. You’re using it as a delivery inspection, performing a final QA check to ensure shipped items meet the required standards. Lot of inspection type 10 is generating during post goods issue. Inspection type 10 lots are generally stock-irrelevant meaning can't control stock quantity or posting, but without usage decision sales representative can not issue stock to customer.

11 – Delivery to Customer Without Sales Order

Before shipping goods to customers. You’re using it as a delivery inspection, performing a final QA check to ensure shipped items meet the required standards. Lot of inspection type 10 is generating during post goods issue but GI without sales order reference.

 

 12 – General Delivery

To trigger an inspection lot when goods are received against a delivery document (often used in inbound logistics, Subcontracting scenarios, Special procurement processes).

13 – Repetitive Manufacturing

Inspection Type 13 is specifically used in Repetitive Manufacturing (REM) environments within SAP.

Its purpose is to integrate quality inspection into the continuous production flow of repetitive manufacturing, where no traditional production orders are used.

14 – Calibration Inspection
This inspection type is designed for calibrating measuring instruments and equipment. It works in conjunction with Plant Maintenance (PM) calibration orders to ensure tools remain accurate and reliable for use.

15 – Sample Management

Inspection type 15 is used specifically for Sample Management in SAP Quality Management. It supports the tracking, testing, and lifecycle of samples to manage and inspect samples that are not directly linked to goods receipt or production. it can be manually created inspection lots or automatically through sample-drawing processes.

16 – Stability Study Inspection
Stability studies or shelf-life studies are conducted to track and examine how different environmental conditions (for example, temperature, brightness, moisture) affect a compound, a material, or a batch over a specified period of time especially critical in pharmaceuticals or FMCG.

17 – Externally Triggered Inspection

In your setup, this inspection type is repurposed for SAP EWM inbound inspections. It's triggered when goods are received from subcontractors or via EWM-managed warehouses, ensuring incoming material quality like inspection type 01.

89 – Manual Inspection Lot
This is a fully manual inspection type used when you want to create inspection lots without relying on automatic triggers inspection lots. Ideal for ad-hoc, special-case, or investigational inspections.

Quality notification

In standard there are three Quality notification but you can customize Quality notification as per your requirement like for OSS, CAPA, Deviation.

Q1 – Customer Complaint

Q1 is a standard Quality Notification Type in SAP used to manage and document customer complaints related to product quality, service, or delivery issues.

Q2 – Complaint against Vendor
A Complaint Against Vendor is a formal quality notification raised in SAP when goods or services received from a supplier (vendor) do not meet the required quality standards, specifications, or contractual terms. This complaint helps document the issue, trigger corrective actions, and maintain quality records for vendor evaluation.

Q3 – Internal Problem

An Internal Problem Notification is a type of quality notification in SAP used to record and manage quality issues identified within the organization, such as during internal inspections, production processes, audits, or any in-house activities. It helps initiate corrective actions, document the issue, and prevent recurrence.

entry.title: Re: Configure a tile on SAP Build Work Zone to manage Decision artifacts
entry.content:
Hi,

i have done the configuration like described but I don't see the app yet in the HTML5 repository.

Any idea ?

entry.title: Re: How to fix Brazil nota fiscal errors while submitting...
entry.content:
Hi, Rajender123. I think you can adjust these fields as follows:
The field 'dFab' (Manufacturing Date) should be in the format "YYYY-MM-DD", 'dVal' (Expiry Date) should be in the format "YYYY-MM-DD" or inform the last day of the month if the validity does not specify the day and the information of the 'pag' (payment) group is not complete.

Thanks and Best Regards!

entry.title: Language Display Requirement in SAC Reports
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have a requirement where some users want the headers (like field names) to be shown in English, but the data content (like descriptions) to be shown in Japanese in the SAC reports.

There are also users who prefer to see both headers and data in Japanese.

Not sure if SAP provides any standard language pack or option to support this kind of setup in SAC.

Please let us know if we have anu suggestions.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Default values in Freight Units for Cross Border Mode of Transport
entry.content: Hi Tim, we have the saame issue as you described. Actually we need to enter the cross border MoT manually as it's not forwarded from the field in the SD document what you showed. Do you got any helpful answer so far to your question? I checked the note 2423784 but it's not helping

entry.content: Release OData MCP Bridge v1.1.3 · oisee/odata_mcp_go - OData V4 support added

entry.content:

Who can I contacts to help me with this task?

entry.title: Customer & Partner Roundtable for SAP BTP ABAP Environment #25
entry.content:
👉 The call for contributions for one of the upcoming roundtables is open! 

 

If you want to show a demo or share a use case scenario for SAP BTP ABAP Environment send us an email and we will get back to you.
 

Introduction

 

As SAP BTP ABAP environment (aka Steampunk) and ABAP Cloud became more popular inside and outside of SAP, there is a high demand for rolling out the latest product news and updates, asking questions, and showing demos. 

💡 You can find the slides presented, recordings, and further references from the previous roundtables in this GitHub repository.

Meeting Information

When:                                                                      
  • July 31, 10:00 - 11:00 AM CET  – Zoom Meeting (please register in advance) 
 
Who:
  • All interested customers, partners, and stakeholders are invited to join this roundtable
  • BTP ABAP team:
    • Frank Jentsch (Product Lead for SAP BTP ABAP Environment)
    • Iwona Hahn (BTP ABAP Product Management)

  • Partner blue-zone GmbH: Andreas Klose (Director Software Development), Christof Unterste (Solution Architect) 

Preliminary Agenda:
  • Product update for SAP BTP ABAP Environment
  • Partner live demo: EDI-ZONE (EDI & eInvoicing Solution) - managed SaaS solution on SAP BTP ABAP Environment:  subaccount handling, customer onboarding etc.
  • Q&A 
Looking forward to meeting you!
 

 



Check out our SAP Business Technology ABAP Environment page in SAP Community for product updates and upcoming events.

entry.title: Use of Settlement Material in Settlement Management
entry.content:
Gurus

I am getting error during settlement that No Material Number Passed On.  I do not have a settlement material in my condition contract but I have successfully created settlement documents for other contracts that do not have a settlement material defined.  What other factors could be causing this error to appear?  And where are the controls to determine if a settlement material is required or not?

This is an External Commission scenario.

Thanks for you help.

David

entry.title: Re: Implementação da NT 2024.003
entry.content:
Olá, Carla_Veloz.

As SAP Notes referentes a NT 2024.003 são:

3560404 - NT 2024.003 v.1.03: Prerequisite Objects for SAP Note 3560477​
3560477 - NT 2024.003 v.1.03 - Nota Fiscal Eletrônica (NF-e): Agriculture and Livestock Tab​

Muito Obrigada!

entry.title: Re: RDP access point connection doesn't work in CAL Appli...
entry.content: Hi Tsventinka,
